title: Understanding Circular Motion
text: When a particle moves along a circular path, it experiences centripetal acceleration. This acceleration is directed towards the center of the circle.

title: Formula for Centripetal Acceleration
text: The formula for centripetal acceleration is given by \(a_c=v**2/r\), where \(v\) is the velocity of the particle and \(r\) is the radius of the circle.

title: Calculate Velocity
text: First, calculate the velocity of the particle. Use the formula \(v=d/t\), where \(d\) is the distance traveled and \(t\) is the time taken. Assume the time taken is $$1$$ second for simplicity.

title: Calculate Centripetal Acceleration
text: Now, use the velocity calculated and the radius of the circle to find the centripetal acceleration using \(a_c=v**2/r\).
